job_title.role: General, Territorial Governor, U.S. Senator, President

resume.summary: William Henry Harrison was the **9th President of the United States (1841)** and a **military hero** from the War of 1812. He served the **shortest presidency in U.S. history**, lasting only **32 days** before his death. Prior to his presidency, he was known for his role as the **Governor of the Indiana Territory** and his military leadership in the **Battle of Tippecanoe (1811)**.

Work_Experience.job_description: Served as the **9th President of the United States**, delivering the **longest inaugural address in history (8,445 words)**, but passed away after **32 days in office** due to pneumonia.
Represented **Ohio** in the U.S. Senate, focusing on **land policy, westward expansion, and military funding**.
Advocated for **veteran benefits, military readiness, and Western expansion**.
Oversaw the governance and development of **Indiana Territory**, including land negotiations with Native American tribes and infrastructure projects.

Led **U.S. forces** against Native American resistance at the **Battle of Tippecanoe (1811)** and played a key role in the **War of 1812**, defeating **British forces at the Battle of the Thames (1813)**.

education.majored_in: Medicine (later pursued military and political career instead)

notes: Harrison was the **first president to die in office**, leading to the **25th Amendment** clarifying presidential succession. His legacy remains as a **military leader, frontier governor, and short-lived president**.
